description_long: This object is a classic submachine gun with a wooden stock and a prominent round drum magazine, reminiscent of the Soviet PPSh-41. The receiver and barrel assembly are constructed from dark metal, with visible vent holes along the barrel shroud for cooling. The wooden stock is smoothly contoured and stained a deep brown, providing a sturdy shoulder rest. The drum magazine is large and circular, attached beneath the receiver, and is made of metal. Iron sights are mounted along the top of the barrel, with a simple post front and notch rear configuration. The overall design is robust and utilitarian, suitable for rapid fire in urban environments.
description: A classic submachine gun with a wooden stock, metal receiver, and a large round drum magazine, featuring iron sights and a utilitarian design.
description_view_0: Front view showing the circular drum magazine and the barrel's muzzle, with the iron sight post visible at the top.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the full length of the rifle, the wooden stock, metal receiver, and the attached drum magazine.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the end of the wooden stock and the alignment of the barrel and drum magazine.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the wooden stock, metal receiver, and the drum magazine from the other side.
